topic: ChatGPT prompting strategies and real-world use cases

entries.a: Prompts.chat is the world's largest open-source prompt library for AI, originally created for ChatGPT but compatible with various AI models like Claude, Gemini, Llama, and Mistral.

entries.q: How can I contribute to prompts.chat?
entries.a: You can contribute by adding prompts at prompts.chat/prompts/new, where they will sync automatically to the library.

entries.q: What is the Interactive Book of Prompting?
entries.a: The Interactive Book of Prompting is a free, interactive guide with over 25 chapters that cover basic to advanced techniques in prompt engineering, including chain-of-thought reasoning and few-shot learning.

entries.q: What is the purpose of the 'Prompting for Kids' feature?
entries.a: The 'Prompting for Kids' feature is an interactive, game-based adventure designed to teach children aged 8-14 how to communicate with AI through fun puzzles and stories.

entries.q: How can I self-host my own prompt library?
entries.a: You can self-host a prompt library by using the command 'npx prompts.chat new my-prompt-library' to create a new library, followed by configuration steps for branding and authentication.

entries.q: What integrations are available for prompts.chat?
entries.a: Prompts.chat can be integrated with various tools, including a CLI for command line access, a Claude Code Plugin for AI tools, and it can serve as an MCP server.

entries.q: What types of AI models does prompts.chat support?
entries.a: Prompts.chat supports a variety of AI models, including ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, and Mistral.
